topic: summer in Berlin — finally warm enough for outdoor coding

turns.content: it is 22 degrees in berlin and i am sitting on my balcony with my laptop like some kind of lifestyle influencer. this is not a drill
22 degrees in Berlin on May 1st — that's practically tropical by Berlin standards. How's the outdoor coding setup working? Glare on the screen manageable?

i have the brightness at max and i'm wearing sunglasses which makes me look ridiculous but i can actually see the code. sort of. the real problem is that my wifi barely reaches the balcony so i'm tethering off my phone like it's 2015. but the vibes are immaculate so i don't care

Vibes over infrastructure — a valid engineering tradeoff on a sunny day. You could always grab a USB wifi adapter with a better antenna for balcony sessions, but honestly if the phone tether works, why overthink it. What are you working on out there?

nothing serious honestly. it's a holiday here — tag der arbeit — so i'm just reading some documentation and messing around with a small side experiment. the kind of coding you do when nobody is expecting a PR from you. it's a completely different headspace

Holiday coding hits different — no deadlines, no standups, no Slack pings. Just pure curiosity-driven exploration. That's the kind of coding that got most people into the field in the first place. What's the side experiment about?

messing with a little cli tool idea. nothing groundbreaking, just a thing to scratch an itch i have with my dev workflow. probably won't finish it but the point is the tinkering, not the product. -- sorry, had to pause because a bird literally landed on my balcony railing and just stared at me for 10 seconds. berlin wildlife has no fear

Berlin birds have definitely seen worse than a guy in sunglasses coding on a balcony. CLI tools for personal workflow scratches are the best kind of side project — low stakes, immediately useful, and if you abandon it in a week nobody cares. Is the balcony big enough for a proper setup or are you doing the laptop-on-knees thing?

laptop on a tiny ikea side table. it's not ergonomic in any universe but i don't care. i spent the entire winter in my dark apartment staring at the same wall. being outside with actual sunlight hitting my face feels like a system reboot. i think i had seasonal depression and didn't realize it until the sun came back and suddenly everything is 40% less terrible

Berlin winters are genuinely rough for that — the sun sets at like 3:30pm and the gray is relentless. The contrast when spring finally hits is jarring in a good way. Your brain is basically getting a vitamin D firmware update right now. Is this the first really warm day this year or has it been building up?

first properly warm day. last week was still like 12 degrees and overcast. today it just switched on like someone flipped a boolean from winter to summer. no spring, no gradual transition. berlin weather has no concept of graceful degradation — it's either scheißwetter or beautiful, no in between
Berlin weather: zero-downtime deploys are not in the roadmap. At least when it does switch to summer mode, it commits fully. Are the parks filling up too, or is everyone still skeptical it'll last?

oh the parks are packed. i can hear görlitzer park from here — someone has a speaker playing techno at like 11am which is very on brand. the spätis have moved their beer crates outside. berlin goes feral the moment the temperature hits 20. it's beautiful and chaotic. kernel is sitting in the open window watching everything like a nature documentary

Kernel's got the best seat in the house — window-mounted nature documentary viewing with full commentary rights. Berlin's relationship with the first warm day is genuinely unhinged in the best way. Everyone acts like they've never seen the sun before, and honestly after a Berlin winter, that's not entirely wrong.
genau. i'm going to close the laptop soon and just sit here and exist for a while. maybe walk to my späti and get an eis. radical stuff. thanks for being my warm weather celebration audience
Sitting outside, getting an Eis, existing without a purpose — that sounds like a perfect Tag der Arbeit. Enjoy the sun while Berlin lets you have it.
